target.name: AlixLabs
target.founder: Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, Amin Karimi

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
AlixLabs is a Series A–stage semiconductor company founded by Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, and Amin Karimi, having raised €14.1M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies in Chips & Semiconductors at the same stage—including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, implies a $28M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across the four core private comparables yields a $1.5B implied valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied, as private-company revenue data is not disclosed. These two methods bracket a triangulated range of $24M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $764M.

The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around AlixLabs' technology differentiation, go-to-market readiness, and capital efficiency relative to its peer set. Stage-progression yields a conservative floor based on capital deployed; peer-median reflects current market pricing for frontier chip ventures but assumes comparable risk and market position. Resolution of technical validation and customer traction would narrow this band materially.
